• Procedural Content Generation in Games - Noor Shaker, Julian Togelius, Mark Nelson

• aiorunstars231 - A run() function that handles all the usual boilerplate for startup and graceful shutdown.

• Kap (label: good first issue)
    An open-source screen recorder built with web technology

• Next.js (label: good first issue)
    A minimalistic framework for universal server-rendered React applications

• Hyper (label: good first issue)
    JS/HTML/CSS Terminal

• Neovim (label: good first issue)
    Vim-fork focused on extensibility and agility.

• sociomantic labs - Berlin based company specializing in real-time bidding for online advertising. Main sponsor of the annual D language conference. Has open-sourced large parts of their codebase as part of the tsunami organization.
• Time series machine learning with h2o+timetk Oct 28, 2017
• Deep learning & parameter tuning with mxnet, h2o package in R Jan 30, 2017
• CrossCode - A retro-inspired 2D game set in the distant future. Comes with great game mechanics such as combos, puzzles, skill trees, quests, items and more.
• Miniclip - Collection of a variety of free games that can be played without giving your email. Flash required.
• DietPistars2.5k - Minimal image designed to fit on a 2GB SD card, with tons of configurable settings and scripts.
• CLion - Smart cross-platform IDE for C/C++ that uses CMake as a build system.
• PhpStorm - Lightning-smart PHP IDE with major frameworks support.
• Rider - A cross-platform .NET/Mono IDE.
• RubyMine - An intelligent Ruby IDE that supports many modern frameworks.
• WebStorm - A smart JavaScript IDE that uses the full power of the modern JavaScript ecosystem.
• autosuggestionsstars17.2k - Fish-like fast/unobtrusive autosuggestions for ZSH.
• basexstars2 - Adds several BaseX aliases for simplified usage.
• bdstars339 - Jump back to a specific directory, without doing cd ../../...
• cmd-architectstars81 - Build commands from what's in history and at prompt, move, delete, add command segments and search history with multi-word queries.
• dircyclestars17 - Cycle through the directory stack.
• editing-workbenchstars39 - Adds sane, complex command line editing (e.g. incremental history word completion).
• emojisstars9 - Adds numerous ascii art emojis to your environment in convenient variables.
• favorite-directoriesstars7 - Fast jumps to your favorite directories.
• functionalstars119 - ZSH higher order functions.
• fuzzy-search-and-editstars26 - ZSH plugin for fuzzy searching files and instantly opening a matched file on matched line.
• hintsstars47 - Display glob and parameter flags and other non completable info right under your editing buffer.
• historystars79 - Extend history so that it can be queried by SQL.
• interactive-cdstars226 - Fish-like interactive tab completion for cd.
• iterm-touchbarstars641 - Display iTerm2 feedback in the MacbookPro TouchBar (Current directory, git branch & status).
• konsole-theme-changerstars1 - Toggle konsole theme from ZSH.
• manydots-magicstars36 - A zle tweak for emulating ...'==../..' etc.
• maven-pluginstars0 - A fork of the oh-my-zsh maven plugin.
• mercurialstars3 - Extracted from oh-my-zsh so you can use it without oh-my-zsh.
• morphostars20 - Terminal screen savers written in pure ZSH, and also screen saver framework.
• navigation-toolsstars257 - Adds htop-like kill, directory bookmarks browser, multi-word incremental history searcher and more.
• open-prstars63 - A ZSH plugin to open pull requests from command line.
• opt-pathstars9 - Automatically add ~/opt subpaths to your $PATH.
• osxstars14 - Add some common macOS related aliases and functions.
• plugin-railsstars2 - ZSH plugin for Rails.
• plugin-vscodestars16 - Plugin for Visual Studio Code, a text editor for macOS, Windows, and Linux.
• reentry-hookstars5 - Plugin that re-enters working directory if it has been removed and re-created.
• selectstars11 - Multi-term searched selection list with approximate matching and uniq mode.
• suffix-aliasstars1 - Directly open files in the shell using ZSH's suffix aliases.
• syntax-highlighting-filetypesstars132 - ZSH syntax highlighting with dircolors in realtime.
• syntax-highlightingstars11.8k - Add syntax highlighting to your ZSH. Make sure you load this before zsh-users/zsh-history-substring-search or they will both break.
• t32stars2 - Plugin for the Lauterbach Trace32 toolset. It automatically registers fonts and sets all necessary environment variables to run the t32 toolset.
• travisstars4 - Opens the Travis CI page for the current repo if one exists.

• tarpstars14 - tarp finds functions and methods without direct unit tests in Go source code.
• Awesome Remote Jobstars20.6k - Curated list of awesome remote jobs. A lot of them are looking for Go hackers.

• LINQ to DB (linq2db) - The fastest LINQ database access library offering a simple, lightweight, fast, and type-safe layer between your POCO objects and your database for more than 10 database engines with full SQL support.
• DeviceIdstars283 - Generate a 'device ID' that can be used to uniquely identify a computer.
• Code 2040 - Through a variety of programs and fellowships, Code2040 is a great initiative creating opportunities in tech for Black and Latinx talent.
• Codebar - Codebar is a non-profit initiative that facilitates the growth of a diverse tech community by running regular programming workshops.

• FrameShift Consulting - FrameShift open sources their fantastic Ally Skills Workshop, Conference incident response materials and many other resources allowing to scale creating welcoming, inclusive, diverse cultures.
• Fund Club - Fund Club raises money to numerous initiatives for mental health, diversity, inclusion, anti-harassment and more.
• Kapor Center - The Kapor family of organizations works to remove barriers to Science, Technology, Engineering and Math (STEM) education and tech careers for underrepresented people of color.

• Open Sourcing Mental Illness - Open Sourcing Mental Illness is a non-profit corporation dedicated to raising awareness, educating, and providing resources to support mental wellness in the tech and open source communities.
• ParadigmIQ - Paradigm offers a range of workshops, training and assessments aiding in diversity, inclusion, belonging, retention and more.
• Techtonica - Techtonica partners with tech companies to offer tech apprenticeships with stipends and job placement to Bay Area women and non-binary, feminine-adjacent adults with low incomes.

• Trans*H4CK - Trans*H4CK empowers trans, gender non conforming, agender and nonbinary people through improving access to social services, promoting gender safety and community sustainability while bringing visibility to trans* tech innovators and entrepreneurs.
• Women Who Code - Women Who Code is a global community empowering women to excel in technology fields through resources, job boards, conference scholarships and other member benefits.
• AlterConf - AlterConf is a traveling conference series that provides safe opportunities for marginalized people and those who support them in the tech and gaming industries.
• Bootstrapping Inclusion - By Jason Wong.
• Alfred-Workflowstars2.4k - Python Library for writing Alfred workflows.
